codeReview.summary: The fix only partially addressed the prior scrutiny findings. It hard-coded better fallback text for the non-Error branch of older-history loading, but the actual user-facing path still allows raw Error.message text to surface, and the details header still exposes storage/cache implementation terminology instead of removing or truly productizing it.

issues.description: The catch block still does rr instanceof Error ? err.message : ..., so users can still see raw backend/generic error strings on the older-history load-more path. This directly misses the stated fix requirement to replace the generic older-history failure message with explicit user-facing Leagueback wording and matches the unresolved scrutiny synthesis finding.

The fix maps details.source to Cached match data / Legacy cached match data, but those labels still expose cache/storage implementation concepts in the user-facing details header. The feature required mapping raw source identifiers to intentional product-facing labels or removing them when they are only diagnostic; this badge remains implementation-facing and the latest scrutiny synthesis still flags it as unresolved.

summary: Fail: the fix improved wording in one fallback branch, but it did not close the original failure because raw Error.message text can still reach the older-history UI and the details header still shows cache/storage implementation labels.
